docs.title: Home
docs.text:
ATOMS + QUARKS

QUARKS Survey

Using ALMA's sharpest eyes to explore the birthplaces of massive stars and reveal how their earliest groups assemble.

QUARKS (Querying Underlying mechanisms of massive star formation with ALMA-Resolved gas Kinematics and Structures) follows dense star-forming protoclusters from the ATOMS sample to smaller physical scales, combining target catalogs, continuum products, and multi-scale image atlases to study fragmentation, core formation, gas kinematics, accretion flows, feedback, and the early evolution of massive star-forming regions.

Browse Targets Open Atlas 139 QUARKS targets 156 Band 6 pointings ATOMS Band 3 survey legacy ~1000 au resolved physical scales

Survey Scope

Connecting clump-scale environments to the birthplaces of massive stars

ATOMS established a uniform ALMA Band 3 view of active massive star-forming regions. QUARKS extends this foundation with higher-resolution Band 6 observations, targeting the internal structure and kinematics of dense star-forming protoclusters.

This website is designed as the public entry point for QUARKS products: target information, continuum images and FITS files, source-by-source multi-scale atlases, publications, team information, and contact points for collaboration.

Legacy How the ATOMS Band 3 survey motivates and supports the QUARKS Band 6 program. Targets Basic source properties for the 139-object QUARKS target sample. Continuum Browse continuum images and download corresponding continuum FITS products. Atlas Multi-scale, multi-wavelength image atlas for individual sources. Publications Papers and science results based on ATOMS and QUARKS observations. Contact Find contact information for data questions and collaboration requests.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
Contact

Contact the QUARKS Team

For questions about the QUARKS survey, please contact the team by email.

Science and data questions Survey Products

Use this contact for questions about target information, continuum files, image atlases, and product usage.

Wenyu Jiao - astrojiao@gmail.com Tie Liu - liutie@shao.ac.cn

When possible, please include the relevant source name, product type, and a short description of the question so that the request can be routed quickly.

docs.title: Datapolicy
docs.text: Please add the following sentences in Acknowledgments: This paper uses the following ALMA data: ADS/JAO. ALMA#2021.1.00095.S. ALMA is a partnership of ESO (representing its member states), NSF (USA) and NINS (Japan), together with NRC (Canada), MOST and ASIAA (Taiwan, China), and KASI (Republic of Korea), in cooperation with the Republic of Chile. The Joint ALMA Observatory is operated by ESO, AUI/NRAO, and NAOJ. The MeerKAT telescope is operated by the South African Radio Astronomy Observatory, which is a facility of the National Research Foundation, an agency of the Department of Science and Innovation.

docs.title: Legacy
docs.text:
Legacy

ATOMS Legacy

QUARKS grows out of the ATOMS Band 3 survey, carrying a well-defined sample of massive star-forming regions to the smaller physical scales probed by ALMA Band 6.

01 Band 3 foundation

ATOMS provides the broader molecular-line and continuum view of massive star-forming environments, setting the clump-scale context for the QUARKS follow-up.

02 Sample continuity

QUARKS follows dense protoclusters selected from the ATOMS sample, allowing source-by-source comparisons across wavelength, resolution, and physical scale.

03 Multi-scale science

Together, ATOMS and QUARKS connect clump structure, fragmentation, dense cores, chemistry, kinematics, accretion, outflows, and feedback.

Context

From ATOMS to QUARKS

ATOMS, the ALMA Three-millimeter Observations of Massive Star-forming regions survey, established a Band 3 view of active massive star-forming regions. It is the legacy reference for the larger-scale dense gas, molecular emission, and feedback environment in which massive protoclusters form.

QUARKS builds on that foundation with high-resolution ALMA Band 6 observations. The combined data set is designed for direct multi-scale comparisons: from clump-scale structures in ATOMS to compact continuum fragments, dense cores, and local gas motions resolved by QUARKS.

ATOMS Band 3 view of massive star-forming regions QUARKS Band 6 follow-up toward dense protoclusters Website products Targets, continuum files, source atlases, and papers Browse QUARKS Targets Open Publications

docs.title: Publications
docs.text:
Literature

Publications QUARKS Series

Core papers presenting the QUARKS survey and first science results.

  1. The ALMA-QUARKS Survey. I. Survey Description and Data Reduction 2024 · Liu, Xunchuan; Liu, Tie; Zhu, Lei; Garay, Guido et al. Introduces the QUARKS survey design, target sample, observing strategy, and the first high-resolution ALMA Band 6 view of dense protoclusters selected from the ATOMS legacy.
  2. The ALMA-QUARKS Survey. II. The ACA 1.3 mm Continuum Source Catalog and the Assembly of Dense Gas in Massive Star-Forming Clumps 2024 · Xu, Fengwei; Wang, Ke; Liu, Tie; Zhu, Lei et al. Presents the ACA 1.3 mm continuum source catalog and connects ACA fragment properties with their host clump properties.
  3. The ALMA-QUARKS Survey. III. Clump-to-core Fragmentation and Searches for High-mass Starless Cores 2025 · Yang, Dongting; Liu, Hong-Li; Liu, Tie; Liu, Xunchuan et al. Builds the TM2+ACA core catalog and uses it to study core properties, clump-to-core fragmentation, and high-mass starless core candidates.
QUARKS-related Papers

QUARKS products and connected follow-up analyses.

  1. Evolution of starless cores in massive clumps seen by the ALMA ASHES and QUARKS surveys 2026 · Yang, Dongting; Liu, Hong-li; Qin, Sheng-li et al. Compares ASHES and QUARKS samples to trace how starless cores evolve in massive clump environments.
  2. Tracking Protostellar Variability in Massive Protoclusters with ALMA. I. Insights from QUARKS and MaMMOtH 2026 · Yang, Yuhan; Liu, Tie; Liu, Sheng-Yuan et al. Uses ALMA data to investigate variability signatures in massive protocluster environments.
  3. ALMA-QUARKS view of W49N: Multipolar episodic outflow associated with the most energetic Galactic water maser 2026 · Jiao, Yunfan; Liu, Tie; Jiao, Wenyu et al. Resolves complex episodic outflow activity in W49N and links it to powerful water maser emission.
  4. The ALMA-QUARKS Survey: Evidence of an Explosive Molecular Outflow in IRAS 15520-5234 2026 · Hoque, Ariful; Baug, Tapas; Guzman, Estrella et al. Reports an explosive molecular outflow candidate revealed by QUARKS observations.
  5. The ALMA-QUARKS survey: Investigating Thermal Feedback of Massive Protostars in Hot Molecular Cores 2026 · Meng, Dezhao; Liu, Tie; Esimbek, Jarken et al. Examines how massive protostars heat and reshape their surrounding hot molecular cores.
  6. ATOMS-QUARKS Survey: Inflow and Infall in Massive Protocluster G318.049+00.086 2026 · Gupta, Shivani; Baug, Tapas; Soam, Archana et al. Combines ATOMS and QUARKS views to identify inflow and infall signatures linked to competitive accretion.
  7. The ALMA-QUARKS Survey: Hot Molecular Cores Are a Long-standing Phenomenon in the Evolution of Massive Protostars 2026 · Meng, Dezhao; Liu, Tie; Esimbek, Jarken et al. Studies hot-core occurrence across massive protostellar evolution using QUARKS diagnostics.
  8. ALMA-QUARKS: Few-thousand-year "Hatching out of the Egg" 2026 · Zhang, Siju; Garay, Guido; Xu, Fengwei et al. Interprets a hypercompact H II region breaking out from its parental hot core on a short timescale.
  9. The ALMA-QUARKS Survey: Evidence of a Candidate High-mass Prestellar Core Aside a Bright-rimmed Cloud IRAS 18290-0924 2025 · Yang, Dongting; Liu, Hong-Li; Qin, Sheng-li et al. Identifies and analyzes a candidate high-mass prestellar core in a feedback-affected environment.
  10. The ALMA-ATOMS-QUARKS Survey: Resolving a Chemically Rich Massive Protostellar Outflow 2025 · Zou, Jiahang; Liu, Tie; Xu, Fengwei et al. Links outflow structure and chemistry using combined ATOMS and QUARKS observations.
  11. The ALMA-QUARKS survey: Extensive detection of acetamide in multiple high-mass star-forming regions 2025 · Duan, Chunguo; Xu, Xuefang; Gou, Qian et al. Reports widespread acetamide detections and probes complex chemistry in high-mass star-forming regions.
  12. The ALMA-QUARKS Survey: Fibers' Role in Star Formation Unveiled in an Intermediate-mass Protocluster Region of the Vela D Cloud 2025 · Yang, Dongting; Liu, Hong-Li; Liu, Tie et al. Explores how fiber-like gas structures participate in star formation within Vela D.
  13. The ALMA-QUARKS Survey: Detection of Two Extremely Dense Substructures in a Massive Prestellar Core 2024 · Mai, Xiaofeng; Liu, Tie; Liu, Xunchuan; Zhu, Lei et al. Highlights compact, very dense substructures that may represent early stages of massive core evolution.
Legacy: ATOMS Series

The ATOMS survey provides the Band 3 legacy foundation for QUARKS. The full ATOMS literature is collected through ADS.

Open ATOMS literature in ADS

docs.title: Team
docs.text:
Collaboration

QUARKS Team

The QUARKS collaboration brings together researchers working on massive star formation, ALMA observations, dense gas kinematics, fragmentation, chemistry, feedback, and multi-scale data products.

Consortium Members

Names are sorted alphabetically by family name.
